_cite.reason: URL-normalization 1c: strip trailing markdown-emphasis `*`/backtick glued to citation URLs in intelligence_item_sections.content_md (customer-checkable link + validate_item_provenance criterion-2 exact-compare). Operator ruling 2026-07-04. Same transform as the write-site fix.

prior.content_md:
**MONITORING** — Establish a EUR-Lex alert subscription for all instruments listed in the Confirmed Regulatory Timeline section. EUR-Lex provides a free document-watch function via registered account. Without active alerting, the workspace is dependent on secondary sources for notification of amendments, corrigenda, and delegated act publications — each of which can alter compliance obligations without industry-wide announcement. Assign a named internal owner for each instrument cluster. *Source: EUR-Lex Official Portal, Publications Office of the European Union, https://eur-lex.europa.eu.*

**ACTION REQUIRED** — For the workspace's ocean tertiary operations on European trade lanes: verify that carriers used for EU port calls are reporting under the EU MRV Regulation and are subject to EU ETS from 2024. The workspace may receive ETS cost pass-through surcharges from ocean carriers. Without confirming the underlying allowance prices from the EEA EU ETS Data Viewer, the workspace cannot validate whether surcharge levels are proportionate. *Source: EEA EU ETS Data Viewer, European Environment Agency, https://www.eea.europa.eu/en/analysis/maps-and-charts/emissions-trading-viewer-1-dashboards. Source: EUR-Lex, Regulation (EU) 2023/1805 — FuelEU Maritime, OJ L 234, 22.9.2023.*

**COST ALERT** — ReFuelEU Aviation is in force as of 1 January 2025 (blending obligations on fuel suppliers at EU airports). SAF blending mandates will increase jet fuel costs at EU airports. The workspace's air-primary transport mode on Europe trade lanes is directly exposed to upward fuel cost pressure as SAF blending volumes scale. The workspace does not pay the blending cost directly, but airlines operating on EU routes will pass through increased fuel costs. Timing and magnitude of airline surcharge changes requires carrier-specific data. *Source: Regulation (EU) 2023/2405, EUR-Lex / Official Journal of the European Union, https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg/2023/2405/oj/eng.*

**WINDOW CLOSING** — The EU Fit for 55 package is substantially adopted. Multiple instruments are now in enforcement phase or entering it. The window for the workspace to build internal compliance infrastructure ahead of first enforcement deadlines — particularly on emissions reporting and packaging — is narrowing. The instruments and their deadlines are listed in the Confirmed Regulatory Timeline section. *Source: Council of the European Union, Fit for 55, https://www.consilium.europa.eu/en/policies/fit-for-55/.*

---
